Implement the following plan:

# Lexical Editor Full Upgrade — Implementation Plan

## Context

The iskrib_v3 editor currently has a minimal Lexical setup (5 packages, ~12 features). Writers are missing essential tools like lists, links, markdown shortcuts, and mobile-friendly formatting. This upgrade brings the editor to near-Playground level with features curated for a writing platform, plus a mobile-first toolbar redesign.

**Current state:** Bold, Italic, Underline, H1-H3, Quote, Text/Highlight color, Alignment, Image insert/resize, @Mentions, Undo/Redo (keyboard only)

**After upgrade:** All of the above PLUS bullet/numbered/checklists, links with auto-detection, strikethrough, inline code, code blocks with syntax highlighting, horizontal rules, markdown shortcuts, floating selection toolbar (mobile), image paste/drop, undo/redo buttons, scrollable mobile toolbar

---

## Phase 1 — Shared Config Foundation + Package Install

**Goal:** Install packages, create single source of truth for editor config. Zero UI changes.

### Step 1.1 — Install packages
```
cd client && npm install @lexical/list @lexical/link @lexical/markdown @lexical/code
```

### Step 1.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editorConfig.js`
- Export `EDITOR_NODES` array: ImageNode, MentionNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode, ListNode, ListItemNode, LinkNode, AutoLinkNode, HorizontalRuleNode, CodeNode, CodeHighlightNode
- Export `EDITOR_THEME` object with CSS classes for all new node types (lists, links, code, strikethrough, inline code, checklist, code highlighting tokens)
- Export `VIEWER_NODES` (same as EDITOR_NODES — read-only composers need them to deserialize)

### Step 1.3 — Update all LexicalComposer instances to use shared config
Files to modify:
- `client/src/App.jsx` — replace inline `editorTheme`/`editorConfig` with imports from `editorConfig.js`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` — both read-only composer instances
- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterEditor/ChapterEditor.jsx`
- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterReader/ChapterReader.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/editJournal.jsx`

---

## Phase 2 — Core Features: Lists, Strikethrough, HR, Undo/Redo, Inline Code

**Goal:** Add the most essential missing features writers need.

### Step 2.1 — Register plugins in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
```jsx
import { ListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalListPlugin';
import { CheckListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalCheckListPlugin';
import { HorizontalRulePlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalHorizontalRulePlugin';
```
Also register in `ChapterEditor.jsx`.

### Step 2.2 — Expand `Toolbar.jsx`

New button layout (grouped with dividers):

| Group | Buttons |
|---|---|
| History | Undo, Redo |
| Image | Insert image |
| Inline format | Bold, Italic, Underline, **Strikethrough**, **Inline code** |
| Block type | H1, H2, H3, Quote, **Code block** |
| Lists | **Bullet list**, **Numbered list**, **Checklist** |
| Insert | **Horizontal rule** |
| Color | Text color, Highlight |
| Alignment | Left, Center, Right |

Key additions:
- **Undo/Redo:** Use `UNDO_COMMAND`/`REDO_COMMAND`, track `canUndo`/`canRedo` via `CAN_UNDO_COMMAND`/`CAN_REDO_COMMAND` listeners
- **Lists:** Use `INSERT_UNORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_ORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_CHECK_LIST_COMMAND`, `REMOVE_LIST_COMMAND` from `@lexical/list`
- **Strikethrough/Code:** `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` with `'strikethrough'` / `'code'`
- **HR:** Dispatch `INSERT_HORIZONTAL_RULE_COMMAND`
- **Code block:** `$setBlocksType` with `$createCodeNode()` from `@lexical/code`

Update `DEFAULT_ACTIVE_STATES` to include: `strikethrough`, `inlineCode`, `listType`, `isCodeBlock`, `canUndo`, `canRedo`

Update the `registerUpdateListener` to detect list type (`$isListNode`), code blocks, and new format states.

### Step 2.3 — CSS in `editor.css`

Add classes for:
- `.editor-text-strikethrough` — `text-decoration: line-through`
- `.editor-text-code` — monospace font, subtle background, border-radius
- `.editor-list-ul` / `.editor-list-ol` — proper margins/padding
- `.editor-listitem` — list item spacing
- `.editor-listitem-checked` / `.editor-listitem-unchecked` — checkbox via `::before` pseudo-element
- `.editor-nested-listitem` — `list-style-type: none`
- `.editor-input hr` — styled horizontal rule (border-top, margins)
- `.editor-code` — code block: monospace, background, padding, border-radius, overflow-x auto
- `.editor-link` — accent color, underline, cursor pointer
- Code highlight tokens (`.editor-tokenize-comment`, `.editor-tokenize-keyword`, etc.)

---

## Phase 3 — Mobile Toolbar Redesign

**Goal:** Make toolbar usable on 375px screens.

### Step 3.1 — Switch from flex-wrap to horizontal scroll
```css
.toolbar {
  flex-wrap: nowrap;
  overflow-x: auto;
  scrollbar-width: none;
  -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
}
.toolbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }
```

### Step 3.2 — Touch-friendly sizing (44px min tap targets on mobile)
```css
@media (max-width: 640px) {
  .toolbar-bttns, .is-active {
    padding: 0.55rem;
    min-width: 36px;
    min-height: 36px;
  }
}
```

### Step 3.3 — Right-edge fade indicator on `.toolbar-wrapper::after`

---

## Phase 4 — Links: LinkPlugin + AutoLinkPlugin + Floating Link Editor

**Goal:** Users can insert/edit links with inline popup (not modal).

### Step 4.1 — Register plugins in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
- `LinkPlugin` with URL validation
- `AutoLinkPlugin` with URL regex matcher (auto-converts typed URLs to links)

### Step 4.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingLinkEditor.jsx`
- Listens to selection changes, detects when cursor is inside a `LinkNode`
- Renders via `createPortal` to `document.body` (escapes modal overflow)
- Shows: URL input, Save button, Open in new tab button, Remove link button
- Positioned below selected text using `getBoundingClientRect()`
- z-index: 500 (above editor modal at 130)
- `onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}` to prevent focus loss

### Step 4.3 — Add link button to Toolbar.jsx
- Track `isLink` in active states
- On click: dispatch `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` or open floating link editor

### Step 4.4 — CSS for `.floating-link-editor`, `.floating-link-input`, `.floating-link-actions`

---

## Phase 5 — Floating Selection Toolbar (Critical for Mobile)

**Goal:** Mini toolbar appears above text selection with quick formatting.

### Step 5.1 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx`
- Detects non-collapsed range selection via `registerUpdateListener`
- Positions above selection (flips below if near top edge)
- Shows: Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Link, Inline code
- Rendered via `createPortal` (z-index: 600)
- `onMouseDown` prevents deselection
- Viewport edge clamping for horizontal position
- `onLinkClick` prop bridges to FloatingLinkEditor

### Step 5.2 — Register in `RichTextEditor.jsx`

### Step 5.3 — CSS for `.floating-selection-toolbar`, `.fst-btn`, `.fst-active`

---

## Phase 6 — Markdown Shortcuts

**Goal:** Power-user feature — `#` → heading, `**` → bold, `- ` → list, etc.

### Step 6.1 — Register `MarkdownShortcutPlugin` in `RichTextEditor.jsx` and `ChapterEditor.jsx`
```jsx
import { MarkdownShortcutPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalMarkdownShortcutPlugin';
import { TRANSFORMERS } from '@lexical/markdown';
```

Supported shortcuts (built-in): `# ` H1, `## ` H2, `### ` H3, `> ` quote, `- `/`* ` bullet list, `1. ` numbered list, `- [ ] ` checklist, `---` HR, ` ``` ` code block, `**bold**`, `_italic_`, `` `code` ``, `~~strikethrough~~`

---

## Phase 7 — Image Paste/Drop

**Goal:** Drop or paste images directly into the editor.

### Step 7.1 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/PasteImagePlugin.jsx`
- Attaches `paste` and `drop` event listeners on editor root element
- Checks `clipboardData.files` / `dataTransfer.files` for images
- Reuses existing upload pattern: blob URL → INSERT_IMAGE_COMMAND → upload → replace src
- Receives `addUploadedImagePath` and `token` as props

### Step 7.2 — Register in `RichTextEditor.jsx` and `ChapterEditor.jsx`

---

## Phase 8 — Code Blocks with Syntax Highlighting

### Step 8.1 — Register `CodeHighlightPlugin` in `RichTextEditor.jsx` and `ChapterEditor.jsx`

### Step 8.2 — Code block toggle in Toolbar.jsx
- Detect `isCodeBlock` when cursor is inside `CodeNode`
- Toggle via `$setBlocksType` with `$createCodeNode()` / `$createParagraphNode()`

---

## Phase 9 — Cleanup: Consolidate ToolbarForEdit

### Step 9.1 — Switch `editJournal.jsx` to use main `Toolbar.jsx`
- The main Toolbar is now feature-complete and superior
- `ToolbarForEdit.jsx` can be deleted after verification

### Step 9.2 — Register all new plugins in `editJournal.jsx`

---

## New Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editorConfig.js` | Shared nodes, theme, config |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingLinkEditor.jsx` | Inline link edit popup |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx` | Mini toolbar on text selection |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/PasteImagePlugin.jsx` | Paste/drop image handling |

## Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `client/package.json` | Add @lexical/list, @lexical/link, @lexical/markdown, @lexical/code |
| `client/src/App.jsx` | Import from editorConfig.js |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/Toolbar.jsx` | Major: add ~10 new buttons, undo/redo, list/code/link detection |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/RichTextEditor.jsx` | Register ~8 new plugins |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css` | All new CSS classes + mobile toolbar scroll |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` | Use VIEWER_NODES/EDITOR_THEME |
| `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterEditor/ChapterEditor.jsx` | Use shared config + new plugins |
| `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterReader/ChapterReader.jsx` | Use VIEWER_NODES/EDITOR_THEME |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/editJournal.jsx` | Switch to main Toolbar, shared config |

## File to Delete

| File | Reason |
|---|---|
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/ToolbarForEdit.jsx` | Replaced by main Toolbar.jsx |

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Lists:** Create bullet, numbered, and checklist items. Nest lists. Save as draft, reopen — verify content preserved. View published post — verify lists render correctly in ContentView.
2. **Links:** Type a URL — verify auto-link. Select text, click link button — verify floating editor appears. Edit URL, remove link. Save and verify links render clickable in read-only view.
3. **Strikethrough/Code:** Select text, apply each format. Verify visual styling. Verify persistence across save/load.
4. **HR:** Insert horizontal rule. Verify styling. Verify in ContentView read-only rendering.
5. **Undo/Redo:** Click undo/redo buttons. Verify they work. Verify disabled state when nothing to undo/redo.
6. **Markdown shortcuts:** Type `# ` → H1, `- ` → bullet list, `**text**` → bold, `` `code` `` → inline code, `---` → HR, ``` ``` ``` → code block.
7. **Floating toolbar:** Select text on mobile — verify toolbar appears. Apply formatting. Verify toolbar disappears on deselect.
8. **Image paste/drop:** Paste image from clipboard into editor. Drag image file into editor. Verify upload and rendering.
9. **Code blocks:** Create code block. Type code. Verify syntax highlighting. Toggle back to paragraph.
10. **Mobile:** Test full toolbar scroll on 375px width. Verify all buttons accessible via scroll. Verify floating toolbar positioning.
11. **Backward compat:** Open an existing old post for editing — verify it loads correctly with no errors.
12. **ChapterEditor:** Verify all new features work in story chapter editing flow.


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\a2369f45-d487-4a0a-8d62-7610b6c3bd69.jsonl

# iskrib_v3 Project Memory

## Architecture
- **Frontend:** React + Vite, BrowserRouter, Lexical rich text editor
- **Backend:** Express.js on port 3000, Supabase (PostgreSQL + Storage)
- **State:** React Query for caching, Context for auth
- **Styling:** CSS variables, glass morphism, Framer Motion animations

## Key Patterns
- Single `<LexicalComposer>` wraps entire app in `App.jsx` (nodes: ImageNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode)
- New independent editors need their own `<LexicalComposer>` instance
- API calls use `fetch` with Bearer token auth, FormData for file uploads
- Backend validates via service layer, controllers are thin wrappers
- Controllers use `asyncHandler` wrapper from `server/utils/controllerHandler.js` (auto try/catch)
- Services throw `AppError` from `server/utils/AppError.js` (proper Error subclass with status/message)
- Shared validation: `server/utils/validation.js` (UUID_REGEX, USERNAME_REGEX, RESERVED_USERNAMES)
- Client API uses helpers from `client/API/apiHelpers.js` (publicGet, authedGet, authedJsonRequest, authedFormRequest)
- Profile customization stored in `profile_layout` JSON column on `users` table
- Image uploads: multer → Sharp (resize per bucket + WebP) → Supabase Storage
- `BUCKET_RESIZE_LIMITS` in routes.js: avatars 400x400, background 1920x1080, story-covers 800x1200, journal-images 1600x1600

# iskrib_v3 Project Memory

## Architecture
- **Frontend:** React + Vite, BrowserRouter, Lexical rich text editor
- **Backend:** Express.js on port 3000, Supabase (PostgreSQL + Storage)
- **State:** React Query for caching, Context for auth
- **Styling:** CSS variables, glass morphism, Framer Motion animations

## Key Patterns
- Single `<LexicalComposer>` wraps entire app in `App.jsx` (nodes: ImageNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode)
- New independent editors need their own `<LexicalComposer>` instance
- API calls use `fetch` with Bearer token auth, FormData for file uploads
- Backend validates via service layer, controllers are thin wrappers
- Controllers use `asyncHandler` wrapper from `server/utils/controllerHandler.js` (auto try/catch)
- Services throw `AppError` from `server/utils/AppError.js` (proper Error subclass with status/message)
- Shared validation: `server/utils/validation.js` (UUID_REGEX, USERNAME_REGEX, RESERVED_USERNAMES)
- Client API uses helpers from `client/API/apiHelpers.js` (publicGet, authedGet, authedJsonRequest, authedFormRequest)
- Profile customization stored in `profile_layout` JSON column on `users` table
- Image uploads: multer → Sharp (resize per bucket + WebP) → Supabase Storage
- `BUCKET_RESIZE_LIMITS` in routes.js: avatars 400x400, background 1920x1080, story-covers 800x1200, journal-images 1600x1600
- `getUserDataService` uses explicit `USER_PROFILE_SELECT` (excludes `interests_embedding`)
- QueryClient defaults: staleTime 5min, refetchOnWindowFocus false
- Share image routes use in-memory LRU cache (1h TTL, 200 entries)
- `getDynamicFloor()` in discoveryService cached with 5min TTL
- Query keys use `session?.user?.id` not `access_token` (prevents cache bust on token rotation)
- Feed queries use `JOURNAL_METADATA_WITH_COUNTS_SELECT` (no `content` field) — previews via `preview_text`/`thumbnail_url` columns
- `preview_text`/`thumbnail_url` persisted at write time in uploadService.js, backfilled via `server/scripts/backfillPreviews.js`
- Edit from profile fetches full content via `GET /journal/:id/content` (auth-required, owner-only)
- ContentView gate: `shouldFetchPost` checks `!statePostData?.content` to trigger fetch when navigating from feed
- `getJournalByIdService` still returns full `content` for single-post detail views
- `attachRepostSources` still returns `content` + `preview_text` + `thumbnail_url` for repost source rendering

## File Locations
- Routes: `server/routes/routes.js` (thin — all handlers in controllers)
- Sitemap routes: `server/routes/sitemapRoutes.js`
- Upload services: `server/services/uploadService.js`
- Notification service: `server/services/notificationService.js`
- Controllers: notificationController, opinionController, followController, inlineController (views, privacy, replies, username, streak)
- Utilities: `server/utils/AppError.js`, `server/utils/controllerHandler.js`, `server/utils/validation.js`
- URL utils (SITE_URL, makePostUrl): `server/utils/urlUtils.js`
- Client API: `client/API/Api.js`, `client/API/apiHelpers.js`
- SQL migrations: `server/sql/interaction_indexes.sql`, `server/sql/follow_counts.sql`, `server/sql/notification_count.sql`
- SEO config: `client/src/seo/seoConfig.js`
- SEO DOM helpers: `client/src/seo/seoUtils.js`
- SEO manager: `client/src/seo/SeoManager.jsx`
- Post SEO hook: `client/src/seo/usePostSeo.js`
- Profile SEO hook: `client/src/seo/useProfileSeo.js`
- Profile layout: `client/src/utils/profileLayout/`
- Profile page: `client/src/components/ProfilePage/`
- Visited profile: `client/src/components/VisitProfile/`
- Editor: `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/`

## SEO Features (Feb 2026)
- Dynamic sitemaps: `/api/sitemap-index.xml`, `/api/sitemap-posts.xml`, `/api/sitemap-profiles.xml`
- Post pages get Article JSON-LD, proper og:type/title/description/image via `usePostSeo` hook
- Profile pages get Person JSON-LD via `useProfileSeo` hook
- `/@username` routes added alongside legacy `/visitProfile?userId=` (backward compatible)
- `username` column on `users` table (unique, case-insensitive)
- Backfill script: `server/scripts/backfillUsernames.js`
- Username endpoints: `GET /api/user/:username`, `GET /api/check-username/:username`, `POST /api/update-username`
- Signup flow includes username field (auto-suggested from name, real-time availability check)
- `handleClickProfile` accepts optional `clickedUsername` param for `/@username` navigation
- `seoConfig.js` DYNAMIC_PATTERNS support function-based seo (receives pathname)

## Notes Feature (Feb 2026)
- Added customizable notes section to profile pages
- Data in `profile_layout.notes` array, max 10 notes
- Dedicated endpoint `POST /updateProfileLayout` (avoids name/bio validation)
- Each note has its own LexicalComposer instance
- Components: ProfileNotesSection, NoteContainer, NoteStyleEditor, InlineNoteEditor, ReadOnlyNoteView

## Stories Feature (Feb 2026) - Phase 1+2
- Wattpad-style serialized content with chapters + paragraph-level comments
- DB tables: stories, chapters, story_comments, story_votes, reading_progress, story_library
- Migration SQL: `server/sql/stories.sql`
- Storage bucket: `story-covers`
- Backend: `server/services/storyService.js`, `chapterService.js`, `storyInteractService.js`
- Controllers: `server/controller/storyController.js`, `chapterController.js`, `storyInteractController.js`
- Routes: `server/routes/storyRoutes.js` (mounted in server.js via `/api`)
- Client API: `client/API/StoryApi.js`
- Hooks: `client/src/components/Stories/hooks/useStoryData.js`, `useStoryMutations.js`
- Components built (Phase 2): StoryDashboard, StoryEditor, StoryChapterManager, ChapterEditor
- Routes: `/home/stories/dashboard`, `/home/stories/new`, `/:storyId/edit`, `/:storyId/manage`, `/:storyId/chapter/:chapterId/edit`
- Sidebar "Stories" link added to Home.jsx (book icon)
- Future phases: StoryBrowser, StoryDetail, ChapterReader, paragraph comments, profile tabs, SEO

## Discovery Feature (Feb 2026) - Phase 1
- Related posts engine using pgvector cosine similarity + engagement + recency composite scoring
- SQL RPC: `find_related_posts` in `server/sql/discovery.sql`
- Backend: `server/services/discoveryService.js`, `server/controller/discoveryController.js`
- Route: `GET /journal/:journalId/related` (no auth required, public posts only)
- Client API: `getRelatedPosts()` in `client/API/Api.js`
- Component: `client/src/components/Discovery/RelatedPosts.jsx` + CSS
- Integrated into `ContentView.jsx` after `</article>` tag
- Confidence tiers: high (>=0.55, 5 results), medium (>=0.40, 3 results), low (>=0.30, 2 results)
- Dynamic similarity floor: <500 posts=0.25, <2K=0.30, 2K+=0.35
- Never shows <2 results or similarity scores; section hidden when no matches
- Future phases: Similar Thinkers (galaxy_edges), Topic Scaffolding, Writing Prompts

## Onboarding Wizard (Mar 2026)
- 5-step wizard replacing old profile editor modal + WelcomeMessage
- DB columns: `onboarding_completed`, `onboarding_completed_at`, `writing_interests`, `writing_goal` on `users` table
- Migration: `server/sql/onboarding.sql` (backfills existing users as onboarded)
- Backend: `completeOnboardingService` in `uploadService.js`, `completeOnboardingController` in `uploadController.js`
- Route: `POST /api/complete-onboarding` (requireAuth)
- `checkUserService` returns `onboarding_completed`; controller returns `onboardingCompleted` field
- Client API: `completeOnboarding()` in `Api.js`
- Components: `client/src/components/Onboarding/` — OnboardingWizard, steps/ (Welcome, ProfileSetup, Interests, FeatureShowcase, FirstAction), components/ (StepProgressBar, TypewriterText)
- CSS: `OnboardingWizard.css` — warm editorial luxury aesthetic (Playfair Display, Lora, amber accents, glass morphism)
- Home.jsx: `showOnboarding` state, `checkOnboarding` useEffect, `handleOnboardingComplete` callback
- `userExists` prop skips profile step (starts at step 2) for interrupted flows
- Final step CTA adapts to `writingGoal`: journal/publish→write, stories→story, explore→feed

## Mobile App UI Parity (Mar 2026)
- **Location:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\`
- **React Native 0.84.0** with React 19, monorepo under `apps/mobile/`
- **Theme system:** `src/theme/tokens.ts` (light/dark palettes), `ThemeProvider.tsx`, `useThemeStyles.ts`
- **Old `colors.ts` deleted** — all 20+ screens migrated to `useTheme()` hook
- **Typography:** 4 font families (Outfit, Lora, Lexend Deca, Playfair Display) in `assets/fonts/`, linked via `react-native.config.js`
- **Type scale:** `src/theme/typography.ts` — hero, h1-h3, body, ui, caption, button presets
- **Spacing/shadows:** `src/theme/spacing.ts` — spacing tokens, radii, platform-aware shadows
- **Icons:** `src/components/icons/Icon.tsx` — 20 SVG icons via react-native-svg (Heart, Comment, Bookmark, etc.)
- **Core components:** Avatar, PostCard+ActionBar, SearchInput, Chip, GlassCard, SkeletonLoader, EmptyState, Toast
- **LexicalRenderer:** `src/lib/content/LexicalRenderer.tsx` — walks Lexical JSON tree, renders native Text/View/Image
- **Auth screens:** Branded ISKRIB header, gold focus rings, username field on signup, ForgotPasswordScreen
- **Home feed:** PostCard integration, branded header, filter Chips, FAB write button
- **Profile:** Glass card stats, badge pills, colored header bg, avatar with badge ring
- **Notifications:** Avatar + icon overlay, unread gold dot, tap-to-navigate
- **Explore:** SearchInput, tab filters (All/Users/Posts), avatar user rows
- **RelatedPosts:** Horizontal scroll cards below post content
- **Tab bar:** Icons (Home/Compass/Book/Bell/User) with amber active tint
- **Nav types:** Extended with VisitProfile, EditProfile, Settings, FollowList
- **Completed phases:** 0 (Foundation), 1 (Auth), 2 (Feed), 3 (Content), 4A (Profile), 6 (Discovery/Notifs/Explore), 9C (Tab Icons)
- **Remaining phases:** 4B-D (VisitProfile, EditProfile, image picker), 5 (Social), 7 (Stories polish), 8 (Animations), 9A-B (Settings), 10 (Missing features)

1→# Lexical Editor Full Upgrade — Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→The iskrib_v3 editor currently has a minimal Lexical setup (5 packages, ~12 features). Writers are missing essential tools like lists, links, markdown shortcuts, and mobile-friendly formatting. This upgrade brings the editor to near-Playground level with features curated for a writing platform, plus a mobile-first toolbar redesign.
     6→
     7→**Current state:** Bold, Italic, Underline, H1-H3, Quote, Text/Highlight color, Alignment, Image insert/resize, @Mentions, Undo/Redo (keyboard only)
     8→
     9→**After upgrade:** All of the above PLUS bullet/numbered/checklists, links with auto-detection, strikethrough, inline code, code blocks with syntax highlighting, horizontal rules, markdown shortcuts, floating selection toolbar (mobile), image paste/drop, undo/redo buttons, scrollable mobile toolbar
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## Phase 1 — Shared Config Foundation + Package Install
    14→
    15→**Goal:** Install packages, create single source of truth for editor config. Zero UI changes.
    16→
    17→### Step 1.1 — Install packages
    18→```
    19→cd client && npm install @lexical/list @lexical/link @lexical/markdown @lexical/code
    20→```
    21→
    22→### Step 1.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editorConfig.js`
    23→- Export `EDITOR_NODES` array: ImageNode, MentionNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode, ListNode, ListItemNode, LinkNode, AutoLinkNode, HorizontalRuleNode, CodeNode, CodeHighlightNode
    24→- Export `EDITOR_THEME` object with CSS classes for all new node types (lists, links, code, strikethrough, inline code, checklist, code highlighting tokens)
    25→- Export `VIEWER_NODES` (same as EDITOR_NODES — read-only composers need them to deserialize)
    26→
    27→### Step 1.3 — Update all LexicalComposer instances to use shared config
    28→Files to modify:
    29→- `client/src/App.jsx` — replace inline `editorTheme`/`editorConfig` with imports from `editorConfig.js`
    30→- `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` — both read-only composer instances
    31→- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterEditor/ChapterEditor.jsx`
    32→- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterReader/ChapterReader.jsx`
    33→- `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/editJournal.jsx`
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Phase 2 — Core Features: Lists, Strikethrough, HR, Undo/Redo, Inline Code
    38→
    39→**Goal:** Add the most essential missing features writers need.
    40→
    41→### Step 2.1 — Register plugins in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
    42→```jsx
    43→import { ListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalListPlugin';
    44→import { CheckListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalCheckListPlugin';
    45→import { HorizontalRulePlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalHorizontalRulePlugin';
    46→```
    47→Also register in `ChapterEditor.jsx`.
    48→
    49→### Step 2.2 — Expand `Toolbar.jsx`
    50→
    51→New button layout (grouped with dividers):
    52→
    53→| Group | Buttons |
    54→|---|---|
    55→| History | Undo, Redo |
    56→| Image | Insert image |
    57→| Inline format | Bold, Italic, Underline, **Strikethrough**, **Inline code** |
    58→| Block type | H1, H2, H3, Quote, **Code block** |
    59→| Lists | **Bullet list**, **Numbered list**, **Checklist** |
    60→| Insert | **Horizontal rule** |
    61→| Color | Text color, Highlight |
    62→| Alignment | Left, Center, Right |
    63→
    64→Key additions:
    65→- **Undo/Redo:** Use `UNDO_COMMAND`/`REDO_COMMAND`, track `canUndo`/`canRedo` via `CAN_UNDO_COMMAND`/`CAN_REDO_COMMAND` listeners
    66→- **Lists:** Use `INSERT_UNORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_ORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_CHECK_LIST_COMMAND`, `REMOVE_LIST_COMMAND` from `@lexical/list`
    67→- **Strikethrough/Code:** `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` with `'strikethrough'` / `'code'`
    68→- **HR:** Dispatch `INSERT_HORIZONTAL_RULE_COMMAND`
    69→- **Code block:** `$setBlocksType` with `$createCodeNode()` from `@lexical/code`
    70→
    71→Update `DEFAULT_ACTIVE_STATES` to include: `strikethrough`, `inlineCode`, `listType`, `isCodeBlock`, `canUndo`, `canRedo`
    72→
    73→Update the `registerUpdateListener` to detect list type (`$isListNode`), code blocks, and new format states.
    74→
    75→### Step 2.3 — CSS in `editor.css`
    76→
    77→Add classes for:
    78→- `.editor-text-strikethrough` — `text-decoration: line-through`
    79→- `.editor-text-code` — monospace font, subtle background, border-radius
    80→- `.editor-list-ul` / `.editor-list-ol` — proper margins/padding
    81→- `.editor-listitem` — list item spacing
    82→- `.editor-listitem-checked` / `.editor-listitem-unchecked` — checkbox via `::before` pseudo-element
    83→- `.editor-nested-listitem` — `list-style-type: none`
    84→- `.editor-input hr` — styled horizontal rule (border-top, margins)
    85→- `.editor-code` — code block: monospace, background, padding, border-radius, overflow-x auto
    86→- `.editor-link` — accent color, underline, cursor pointer
    87→- Code highlight tokens (`.editor-tokenize-comment`, `.editor-tokenize-keyword`, etc.)
    88→
    89→---
    90→
    91→## Phase 3 — Mobile Toolbar Redesign
    92→
    93→**Goal:** Make toolbar usable on 375px screens.
    94→
    95→### Step 3.1 — Switch from flex-wrap to horizontal scroll
    96→```css
    97→.toolbar {
    98→  flex-wrap: nowrap;
    99→  overflow-x: auto;
   100→  scrollbar-width: none;
   101→  -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
   102→}
   103→.toolbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }
   104→```
   105→
   106→### Step 3.2 — Touch-friendly sizing (44px min tap targets on mobile)
   107→```css
   108→@media (max-width: 640px) {
   109→  .toolbar-bttns, .is-active {
   110→    padding: 0.55rem;
   111→    min-width: 36px;
   112→    min-height: 36px;
   113→  }
   114→}
   115→```
   116→
   117→### Step 3.3 — Right-edge fade indicator on `.toolbar-wrapper::after`
   118→
   119→---
   120→
   121→## Phase 4 — Links: LinkPlugin + AutoLinkPlugin + Floating Link Editor
   122→
   123→**Goal:** Users can insert/edit links with inline popup (not modal).
   124→
   125→### Step 4.1 — Register plugins in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
   126→- `LinkPlugin` with URL validation
   127→- `AutoLinkPlugin` with URL regex matcher (auto-converts typed URLs to links)
   128→
   129→### Step 4.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingLinkEditor.jsx`
   130→- Listens to selection changes, detects when cursor is inside a `LinkNode`
   131→- Renders via `createPortal` to `document.body` (escapes modal overflow)
   132→- Shows: URL input, Save button, Open in new tab button, Remove link button
   133→- Positioned below selected text using `getBoundingClientRect()`
   134→- z-index: 500 (above editor modal at 130)
   135→- `onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}` to prevent focus loss
   136→
   137→### Step 4.3 — Add link button to Toolbar.jsx
   138→- Track `isLink` in active states
   139→- On click: dispatch `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` or open floating link editor
   140→
   141→### Step 4.4 — CSS for `.floating-link-editor`, `.floating-link-input`, `.floating-link-actions`
   142→
   143→---
   144→
   145→## Phase 5 — Floating Selection Toolbar (Critical for Mobile)
   146→
   147→**Goal:** Mini toolbar appears above text selection with quick formatting.
   148→
   149→### Step 5.1 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx`
   150→- Detects non-collapsed range selection via `registerUpdateListener`
   151→- Positions above selection (flips below if near top edge)
   152→- Shows: Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Link, Inline code
   153→- Rendered via `createPortal` (z-index: 600)
   154→- `onMouseDown` prevents deselection
   155→- Viewport edge clamping for horizontal position
   156→- `onLinkClick` prop bridges to FloatingLinkEditor
   157→
   158→### Step 5.2 — Register in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
   159→
   160→### Step 5.3 — CSS for `.floating-selection-toolbar`, `.fst-btn`, `.fst-active`
   161→
   162→---
   163→
   164→## Phase 6 — Markdown Shortcuts
   165→
   166→**Goal:** Power-user feature — `#` → heading, `**` → bold, `- ` → list, etc.
   167→
   168→### Step 6.1 — Register `MarkdownShortcutPlugin` in `RichTextEditor.jsx` and `ChapterEditor.jsx`
   169→```jsx
   170→import { MarkdownShortcutPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalMarkdownShortcutPlugin';
   171→import { TRANSFORMERS } from '@lexical/markdown';
   172→```
   173→
   174→Supported shortcuts (built-in): `# ` H1, `## ` H2, `### ` H3, `> ` quote, `- `/`* ` bullet list, `1. ` numbered list, `- [ ] ` checklist, `---` HR, ` ``` ` code block, `**bold**`, `_italic_`, `` `code` ``, `~~strikethrough~~`
   175→

   178→## Phase 7 — Image Paste/Drop
   179→

   182→### Step 7.1 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/PasteImagePlugin.jsx`
   183→- Attaches `paste` and `drop` event listeners on editor root element
   184→- Checks `clipboardData.files` / `dataTransfer.files` for images

# Lexical Editor Full Upgrade — Implementation Plan

## Context

The iskrib_v3 editor currently has a minimal Lexical setup (5 packages, ~12 features). Writers are missing essential tools like lists, links, markdown shortcuts, and mobile-friendly formatting. This upgrade brings the editor to near-Playground level with features curated for a writing platform, plus a mobile-first toolbar redesign.

**Current state:** Bold, Italic, Underline, H1-H3, Quote, Text/Highlight color, Alignment, Image insert/resize, @Mentions, Undo/Redo (keyboard only)

**After upgrade:** All of the above PLUS bullet/numbered/checklists, links with auto-detection, strikethrough, inline code, code blocks with syntax highlighting, horizontal rules, markdown shortcuts, floating selection toolbar (mobile), image paste/drop, undo/redo buttons, scrollable mobile toolbar

---

## Phase 1 — Shared Config Foundation + Package Install

**Goal:** Install packages, create single source of truth for editor config. Zero UI changes.

### Step 1.1 — Install packages
```
cd client && npm install @lexical/list @lexical/link @lexical/markdown @lexical/code
```

### Step 1.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editorConfig.js`
- Export `EDITOR_NODES` array: ImageNode, MentionNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode, ListNode, ListItemNode, LinkNode, AutoLinkNode, HorizontalRuleNode, CodeNode, CodeHighlightNode
- Export `EDITOR_THEME` object with CSS classes for all new node types (lists, links, code, strikethrough, inline code, checklist, code highlighting tokens)
- Export `VIEWER_NODES` (same as EDITOR_NODES — read-only composers need them to deserialize)

### Step 1.3 — Update all LexicalComposer instances to use shared config
Files to modify:
- `client/src/App.jsx` — replace inline `editorTheme`/`editorConfig` with imports from `editorConfig.js`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` — both read-only composer instances
- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterEditor/ChapterEditor.jsx`
- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterReader/ChapterReader.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/editJournal.jsx`

---

## Phase 2 — Core Features: Lists, Strikethrough, HR, Undo/Redo, Inline Code

**Goal:** Add the most essential missing features writers need.

### Step 2.1 — Register plugins in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
```jsx
import { ListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalListPlugin';
import { CheckListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalCheckListPlugin';
import { HorizontalRulePlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalHorizontalRulePlugin';
```
Also register in `ChapterEditor.jsx`.

### Step 2.2 — Expand `Toolbar.jsx`

New button layout (grouped with dividers):

| Group | Buttons |
|---|---|
| History | Undo, Redo |
| Image | Insert image |
| Inline format | Bold, Italic, Underline, **Strikethrough**, **Inline code** |
| Block type | H1, H2, H3, Quote, **Code block** |
| Lists | **Bullet list**, **Numbered list**, **Checklist** |
| Insert | **Horizontal rule** |
| Color | Text color, Highlight |
| Alignment | Left, Center, Right |

Key additions:
- **Undo/Redo:** Use `UNDO_COMMAND`/`REDO_COMMAND`, track `canUndo`/`canRedo` via `CAN_UNDO_COMMAND`/`CAN_REDO_COMMAND` listeners
- **Lists:** Use `INSERT_UNORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_ORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_CHECK_LIST_COMMAND`, `REMOVE_LIST_COMMAND` from `@lexical/list`
- **Strikethrough/Code:** `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` with `'strikethrough'` / `'code'`
- **HR:** Dispatch `INSERT_HORIZONTAL_RULE_COMMAND`
- **Code block:** `$setBlocksType` with `$createCodeNode()` from `@lexical/code`

Update `DEFAULT_ACTIVE_STATES` to include: `strikethrough`, `inlineCode`, `listType`, `isCodeBlock`, `canUndo`, `canRedo`

Update the `registerUpdateListener` to detect list type (`$isListNode`), code blocks, and new format states.

### Step 2.3 — CSS in `editor.css`

Add classes for:
- `.editor-text-strikethrough` — `text-decoration: line-through`
- `.editor-text-code` — monospace font, subtle background, border-radius
- `.editor-list-ul` / `.editor-list-ol` — proper margins/padding
- `.editor-listitem` — list item spacing
- `.editor-listitem-checked` / `.editor-listitem-unchecked` — checkbox via `::before` pseudo-element
- `.editor-nested-listitem` — `list-style-type: none`
- `.editor-input hr` — styled horizontal rule (border-top, margins)
- `.editor-code` — code block: monospace, background, padding, border-radius, overflow-x auto
- `.editor-link` — accent color, underline, cursor pointer
- Code highlight tokens (`.editor-tokenize-comment`, `.editor-tokenize-keyword`, etc.)

---

## Phase 3 — Mobile Toolbar Redesign

**Goal:** Make toolbar usable on 375px screens.

### Step 3.1 — Switch from flex-wrap to horizontal scroll
```css
.toolbar {
  flex-wrap: nowrap;
  overflow-x: auto;
  scrollbar-width: none;
  -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
}
.toolbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }
```

### Step 3.2 — Touch-friendly sizing (44px min tap targets on mobile)
```css
@media (max-width: 640px) {
  .toolbar-bttns, .is-active {
    padding: 0.55rem;
    min-width: 36px;
    min-height: 36px;
  }
}
```

### Step 3.3 — Right-edge fade indicator on `.toolbar-wrapper::after`

---

## Phase 4 — Links: LinkPlugin + AutoLinkPlugin + Floating Link Editor

**Goal:** Users can insert/edit links with inline popup (not modal).

### Step 4.1 — Register plugins in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
- `LinkPlugin` with URL validation
- `AutoLinkPlugin` with URL regex matcher (auto-converts typed URLs to links)

### Step 4.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingLinkEditor.jsx`
- Listens to selection changes, detects when cursor is inside a `LinkNode`
- Renders via `createPortal` to `document.body` (escapes modal overflow)
- Shows: URL input, Save button, Open in new tab button, Remove link button
- Positioned below selected text using `getBoundingClientRect()`
- z-index: 500 (above editor modal at 130)
- `onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}` to prevent focus loss

### Step 4.3 — Add link button to Toolbar.jsx
- Track `isLink` in active states
- On click: dispatch `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` or open floating link editor

### Step 4.4 — CSS for `.floating-link-editor`, `.floating-link-input`, `.floating-link-actions`

---

## Phase 5 — Floating Selection Toolbar (Critical for Mobile)

**Goal:** Mini toolbar appears above text selection with quick formatting.

### Step 5.1 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx`
- Detects non-collapsed range selection via `registerUpdateListener`
- Positions above selection (flips below if near top edge)
- Shows: Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Link, Inline code
- Rendered via `createPortal` (z-index: 600)
- `onMouseDown` prevents deselection
- Viewport edge clamping for horizontal position
- `onLinkClick` prop bridges to FloatingLinkEditor

### Step 5.2 — Register in `RichTextEditor.jsx`

### Step 5.3 — CSS for `.floating-selection-toolbar`, `.fst-btn`, `.fst-active`

---

## Phase 6 — Markdown Shortcuts

**Goal:** Power-user feature — `#` → heading, `**` → bold, `- ` → list, etc.

### Step 6.1 — Register `MarkdownShortcutPlugin` in `RichTextEditor.jsx` and `ChapterEditor.jsx`
```jsx
import { MarkdownShortcutPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalMarkdownShortcutPlugin';
import { TRANSFORMERS } from '@lexical/markdown';
```

Supported shortcuts (built-in): `# ` H1, `## ` H2, `### ` H3, `> ` quote, `- `/`* ` bullet list, `1. ` numbered list, `- [ ] ` checklist, `---` HR, ` ``` ` code block, `**bold**`, `_italic_`, `` `code` ``, `~~strikethrough~~`

---

## Phase 7 — Image Paste/Drop

**Goal:** Drop or paste images directly into the editor.

### Step 7.1 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/PasteImagePlugin.jsx`
- Attaches `paste` and `drop` event listeners on editor root element
- Checks `clipboardData.files` / `dataTransfer.files` for images
- Reuses existing upload pattern: blob URL → INSERT_IMAGE_COMMAND → upload → replace src
- Receives `addUploadedImagePath` and `token` as props

### Step 7.2 — Register in `RichTextEditor.jsx` and `ChapterEditor.jsx`

---

## Phase 8 — Code Blocks with Syntax Highlighting

### Step 8.1 — Register `CodeHighlightPlugin` in `RichTextEditor.jsx` and `ChapterEditor.jsx`

### Step 8.2 — Code block toggle in Toolbar.jsx
- Detect `isCodeBlock` when cursor is inside `CodeNode`
- Toggle via `$setBlocksType` with `$createCodeNode()` / `$createParagraphNode()`

---

## Phase 9 — Cleanup: Consolidate ToolbarForEdit

### Step 9.1 — Switch `editJournal.jsx` to use main `Toolbar.jsx`
- The main Toolbar is now feature-complete and superior
- `ToolbarForEdit.jsx` can be deleted after verification

### Step 9.2 — Register all new plugins in `editJournal.jsx`

---

## New Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editorConfig.js` | Shared nodes, theme, config |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingLinkEditor.jsx` | Inline link edit popup |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx` | Mini toolbar on text selection |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/PasteImagePlugin.jsx` | Paste/drop image handling |

## Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `client/package.json` | Add @lexical/list, @lexical/link, @lexical/markdown, @lexical/code |
| `client/src/App.jsx` | Import from editorConfig.js |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/Toolbar.jsx` | Major: add ~10 new buttons, undo/redo, list/code/link detection |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/RichTextEditor.jsx` | Register ~8 new plugins |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css` | All new CSS classes + mobile toolbar scroll |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` | Use VIEWER_NODES/EDITOR_THEME |
| `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterEditor/ChapterEditor.jsx` | Use shared config + new plugins |
| `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterReader/ChapterReader.jsx` | Use VIEWER_NODES/EDITOR_THEME |
| `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/editJournal.jsx` | Switch to main Toolbar, shared config |

## File to Delete

| File | Reason |
|---|---|
| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/ToolbarForEdit.jsx` | Replaced by main Toolbar.jsx |

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Lists:** Create bullet, numbered, and checklist items. Nest lists. Save as draft, reopen — verify content preserved. View published post — verify lists render correctly in ContentView.
2. **Links:** Type a URL — verify auto-link. Select text, click link button — verify floating editor appears. Edit URL, remove link. Save and verify links render clickable in read-only view.
3. **Strikethrough/Code:** Select text, apply each format. Verify visual styling. Verify persistence across save/load.
4. **HR:** Insert horizontal rule. Verify styling. Verify in ContentView read-only rendering.
5. **Undo/Redo:** Click undo/redo buttons. Verify they work. Verify disabled state when nothing to undo/redo.
6. **Markdown shortcuts:** Type `# ` → H1, `- ` → bullet list, `**text**` → bold, `` `code` `` → inline code, `---` → HR, ``` ``` ``` → code block.
7. **Floating toolbar:** Select text on mobile — verify toolbar appears. Apply formatting. Verify toolbar disappears on deselect.
8. **Image paste/drop:** Paste image from clipboard into editor. Drag image file into editor. Verify upload and rendering.
9. **Code blocks:** Create code block. Type code. Verify syntax highlighting. Toggle back to paragraph.
10. **Mobile:** Test full toolbar scroll on 375px width. Verify all buttons accessible via scroll. Verify floating toolbar positioning.
11. **Backward compat:** Open an existing old post for editing — verify it loads correctly with no errors.
12. **ChapterEditor:** Verify all new features work in story chapter editing flow.

`LinkPlugin` does register the command handler. Now the key issue: the `LinkPlugin` is registered inside `RichTextEditor.jsx` (the main editor inner component), but the `Toolbar` dispatches `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` — both use the same `useLexicalComposerContext()`, so the editor instance should be the same.

Let me check more carefully. The `RichTextEditor.jsx` is inside the main write-new-post editor. But the `Toolbar` is also used in `ChapterEditor` and `editJournal` — do those also register `LinkPlugin`?

OK, that's registered too. Let me look closer at what happens when the link button is clicked — the `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` payload when creating is `'https://'`. Let me check how the v0.17.1 LinkPlugin handles that:

input.command: node -e "try { console.log(Boolean(new URL('https://'))); } catch(e) { console.log('THROWS:', e.message); }"

content.content: THROWS: Invalid URL

content.text:
Found it. `new URL('https://')` throws because `https://` is not a valid URL (no hostname). So `validateUrl` returns `false`, and the `LinkPlugin` handler returns `false` — the link is never created.

The fix is simple: when creating a new link from the toolbar, we should either skip validation for the initial placeholder URL, or use a different approach.

## Context

The iskrib_v3 editor currently has a minimal Lexical setup (5 packages, ~12 features). Writers are missing essential tools like lists, links, markdown shortcuts, and mobile-friendly formatting. This upgrade brings the editor to near-Playground level with features curated for a writing platform, plus a mobile-first toolbar redesign.

**Current state:** Bold, Italic, Underline, H1-H3, Quote, Text/Highlight color, Alignment, Image insert/resize, @Mentions, Undo/Redo (keyboard only)

**After upgrade:** All of the above PLUS bullet/numbered/checklists, links with auto-detection, strikethrough, inline code, code blocks with syntax highlighting, horizontal rules, markdown shortcuts, floating selection toolbar (mobile), image paste/drop, undo/redo buttons, scrollable mobile toolbar

---

## Phase 1 — Shared Config Foundation + Package Install

**Goal:** Install packages, create single source of truth for editor config. Zero UI changes.

### Step 1.1 — Install packages
```

```

### Step 1.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editorConfig.js`
- Export `EDITOR_NODES` array: ImageNode, MentionNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode, ListNode, ListItemNode, LinkNode, AutoLinkNode, HorizontalRuleNode, CodeNode, CodeHighlightNode
- Export `EDITOR_THEME` object with CSS classes for all new node types (lists, links, code, strikethrough, inline code, checklist, code highlighting tokens)
- Export `VIEWER_NODES` (same as EDITOR_NODES — read-only composers need them to deserialize)

### Step 1.3 — Update all LexicalComposer instances to use shared config
Files to modify:
- `client/src/App.jsx` — replace inline `editorTheme`/`editorConfig` with imports from `editorConfig.js`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` — both read-only composer instances
- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterEditor/ChapterEditor.jsx`
- `client/src/components/Stories/ChapterReader/ChapterReader.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/editJournal.jsx`

---

## Phase 2 — Core Features: Lists, Strikethrough, HR, Undo/Redo, Inline Code

**Goal:** Add the most essential missing features writers need.

Also register in `ChapterEditor.jsx`.

### Step 2.2 — Expand `Toolbar.jsx`

New button layout (grouped with dividers):

| Group | Buttons |
|---|---|
| History | Undo, Redo |
| Image | Insert image |
| Inline format | Bold, Italic, Underline, **Strikethrough**, **Inline code** |
| Block type | H1, H2, H3, Quote, **Code block** |
| Lists | **Bullet list**, **Numbered list**, **Checklist** |
| Insert | **Horizontal rule** |
| Color | Text color, Highlight |
| Alignment | Left, Center, Right |

Key additions:
- **Undo/Redo:** Use `UNDO_COMMAND`/`REDO_COMMAND`, track `canUndo`/`canRedo` via `CAN_UNDO_COMMAND`/`CAN_REDO_COMMAND` listeners
- **Lists:** Use `INSERT_UNORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_ORDERED_LIST_COMMAND`, `INSERT_CHECK_LIST_COMMAND`, `REMOVE_LIST_COMMAND` from `@lexical/list`
- **Strikethrough/Code:** `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` with `'strikethrough'` / `'code'`
- **HR:** Dispatch `INSERT_HORIZONTAL_RULE_COMMAND`
- **Code block:** `$setBlocksType` with `$createCodeNode()` from `@lexical/code`

Update `DEFAULT_ACTIVE_STATES` to include: `strikethrough`, `inlineCode`, `listType`, `isCodeBlock`, `canUndo`, `canRedo`

Update the `registerUpdateListener` to detect list type (`$isListNode`), code blocks, and new format states.

### Step 2.3 — CSS in `editor.css`

Add classes for:
- `.editor-text-strikethrough` — `text-decoration: line-through`
- `.editor-text-code` — monospace font, subtle background, border-radius
- `.editor-list-ul` / `.editor-list-ol` — proper margins/padding
- `.editor-listitem` — list item spacing
- `.editor-listitem-checked` / `.editor-listitem-unchecked` — checkbox via `::before` pseudo-element
- `.editor-nested-listitem` — `list-style-type: none`
- `.editor-input hr` — styled horizontal rule (border-top, margins)
- `.editor-code` — code block: monospace, background, padding, border-radius, overflow-x auto
- `.editor-link` — accent color, underline, cursor pointer
- Code highlight tokens (`.editor-tokenize-comment`, `.editor-tokenize-keyword`, etc.)

---

## Phase 3 — Mobile Toolbar Redesign

**Goal:** Make toolbar usable on 375px screens.

### Step 3.1 — Switch from flex-wrap to horizontal scroll
```css
.toolbar {
  flex-wrap: nowrap;
  overflow-x: auto;
  scrollbar-width: none;
  -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
}
.toolbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }
```

### Step 3.2 — Touch-friendly sizing (44px min tap targets on mobile)
```css
@media (max-width: 640px) {
  .toolbar-bttns, .is-active {
    padding: 0.55rem;
    min-width: 36px;
    min-height: 36px;
  }
}
```

### Step 3.3 — Right-edge fade indicator on `.toolbar-wrapper::after`

---

## Phase 4 — Links: LinkPlugin + AutoLinkPlugin + Floating Link Editor

**Goal:** Users can insert/edit links with inline popup (not modal).

### Step 4.1 — Register plugins in `RichTextEditor.jsx`
- `LinkPlugin` with URL validation
- `AutoLinkPlugin` with URL regex matcher (auto-converts typed URLs to links)

### Step 4.2 — Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingLinkEditor.jsx`
- Listens to selection changes, detects when cursor is inside a `LinkNode`
- Renders via `createPortal` to `document.body` (escapes modal overflow)
- Shows: URL input, Save button, Open in new tab button, Remove link button
- Positioned below selected text using `getBoundingClientRect()`

- Track `isLink` in active states
- On click: dispatch `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` or open floating link editor

lines: -# Lexical Editor Full Upgrade — Implementation Plan

-The iskrib_v3 editor currently has a minimal Lexical setup (5 packages, ~12 features). Writers are missing essential tools like lists, links, markdown shortcuts, and mobile-friendly formatting. This upgrade brings the editor to near-Playground level with features curated for a writing platform, plus a mobile-first toolbar redesign.

+The link toolbar button (and floating toolbar link button) silently fail. Root cause: when creating a new link, the Toolbar dispatches `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` with payload `'https://'`. The `LinkPlugin` handler calls `validateUrl('https://')`, which runs `new URL('https://')` — this **throws** because `https://` alone is not a valid URL (no hostname). So `validateUrl` returns `false`, the handler returns `false`, and the link is never created.
-**Current state:** Bold, Italic, Underline, H1-H3, Quote, Text/Highlight color, Alignment, Image insert/resize, @Mentions, Undo/Redo (keyboard only)
+Same issue exists in `FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx` which also dispatches `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` with `'https://'`.

-**After upgrade:** All of the above PLUS bullet/numbered/checklists, links with auto-detection, strikethrough, inline code, code blocks with syntax highlighting, horizontal rules, markdown shortcuts, floating selection toolbar (mobile), image paste/drop, undo/redo buttons, scrollable mobile toolbar

-- Export `EDITOR_THEME` object with CSS classes for all new node types (lists, links, code, strikethrough, inline code, checklist, code highlighting tokens)
-- Export `VIEWER_NODES` (same as EDITOR_NODES — read-only composers need them to deserialize)
